calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Browne for and with Debra Williams.

With respect to the Attorney's costs, the Court noted that it got information on all of the costs except for $238.00. Mr. Browne stated that he couldn't find it. The Court raised the issue of whether the payments that were made without a Court order were done before or after the appointment of Ms. Williams and questions why the Personal Representative should receive full statutory fees to the extent fees were paid to others for work that would ordinarily be the responsibility of the Personal Representative which includes the accounting and stated that the normal practice of the Court is to reduce the statutory fees by those amounts. Mr. Browne stated that he submits on the pleadings and that the extraordinary fee request is withdrawn. The First and Final Report and Account, etc. is under submission. The Court informed the Parties that it is likely to reduce the Attorney's costs and reduce the statutory fees by those amounts that are viewed as ordinary expenditures.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Petition (1) To Establish And Fund Testamentary Trust; (2) To Appoint Successor Trustee; (3) For Preliminary Distribution From Testamentary Trust; And (4) For Partial Statutory Fees For Administrator And For Attorney
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Browne for and with Debra Williams (Petitioner/Administrator).

The Court stated that it now has an amended petition, that is does not have a problem with the request to have a trust established and have a named trustee, and to make distribution to the trust, but it is not going to approve the Beneficiaries' agreement and that it will require a petition in a trust case number. The amount of bond will be $4,583,000.00. The Court granted the Petition to Establish and Fund Testamentary Trust, etc. (subject to review of the order). Status on filing of bond is set for July 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition (1) To Establish And Fund Testamentary Trust; (2) To Appoint Successor Trustee; (3) For Preliminary Distribution From Testamentary Trust; And (4) For Partial Statutory Fees For Administrator And For Attorney
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Continued To Jun-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Browne for Petitioner.

The Court noted that at the last hearing it explained that the Court is not going to skip a step, stated that Counsel needs to set up the testamentary trust, and provide an order that sets out the terms of the trust and distributes the estate to that trust. Mr. Browne stated that he does not understand. The Court explained to Mr. Browne that the amended petition can ask for distribution to the trust, but not from the trust, and that distribution from the trust needs to be requested in another petition in a new trust case number. The Court also informed Mr. Browne that it will not approve preliminary fees while this is in such disarray. Mr. Browne stated that he has tried his best and that he thinks he now understands what he needs to do. The Petition to Establish and Fund Testamentary Trust, etc. is continued to June 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing Date; Declaration Of John R. Browne Iii; And Proposed Order
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Denied

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: John R. Browne for Debra Williams.

The Court stated before it today is a Petition for Preliminary Distribution which seeks other relief, that the will sets up a testatmentary trust, and that there is a request to distribute from the will. Mr. Browne stated that Dolores Parker had agreed to a lump sum. The Court stated that the parties want to do something different from what the settlor intended. Mr. Browne addressed Probate Code 11621(a). The Court stated that the will would not be rewritten to ignore the Trust and that it would create bigger issues. Mr. Browne stated that there was no Trustee and that Debra Williams was asking to be appointed Successor Trustee. The Court stated that something needed to be done to fund the Trust. Mr. Browne stated that Hibernia Bank will be the designated Trustee. Brief discussions are held. The Court stated that the Trust doesn't terminate until the last Sister passes away and that the intent of the decedent is clear. Mr. Browne stated that they were asking for $960,000.00 to be distributed. The Court stated that the harm is that the distribution would be coming from the Estate not the Trust. The Court noted that counsel could ask for a preliminary distribution to the Trust, and then DENIED the petition without prejudice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES
Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Continued To Mar-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Giffen (Petitioner).
James Browne for and with Debra Williams (Petitioner).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the appointment of their respective Clients. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Continued To Jan-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Giffen.
John Browne for and with Debra Williams.

The Court stated that it appears Ms. Williams' petition has priority. Ms. Harrington stated that her Client has the same priority as those who nominated her, that they have nominations from the Nieces, provided history, stated that the submission of December 15th says Mr. Browne does not have anything in writing from either subscribing witness, and that Ms. Williams has been acting as Administrator with no legal authority collecting rents (7 properties). Mr. Browne stated that he was unsuccessful in contacting the subscribing witnesses and that the letter of Ellen Bischoff dated October 2, 2000 on the stationery of Mr. McCabe should be sufficient. Ms. Harrington stated that she is not prepared to submit, that she would like to find out who took over Mr. McCabe's practice and whether they have his will files, and that she objects to Ms. Williams' petition as they have not met the requirements of the Probate Code. The Court expressed concern about Ms. Williams acting as Administrator without Court appointment and the solicitation letters sent out by Ms. Harrington. The Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to January 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
